pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 29, 34 explicitly create student with first_name='Sarah'
setup_task.sh lines 29, 34 explicitly create student with last_name='Connor'
task.json description mentions 'Severe Peanut Allergy'; verifier.py line 45 confirms default value 'Peanut'
task.json description mentions 'EpiPen required'; verifier.py line 46 confirms default value 'EpiPen'
direct_db_setup.sh line 11 sets ADMIN_USER='admin'; setup_opensis.sh line 327 confirms 'Username: admin'
direct_db_setup.sh line 12 sets ADMIN_PASS='Admin@123'; setup_opensis.sh line 157 also references 'Admin@123'

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on OpenSIS, a legitimate open-source Student Information System used by thousands of schools. The student being modified is 'Sarah Connor' (Grade 10), created synthetically during task setup in a local OpenSIS database. The medical alert to add must contain the keywords 'Peanut' and 'EpiPen' (from the description 'Severe Peanut Allergy - EpiPen required'). All authentication credentials are hardcoded for the local demo environment: username='admin', password='Admin@123'. The task requires navigating the Student Info module's Medical/Health records section to add the new medical alert with today's date.
